retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

- Board examination

  If you have not passed an exam three times, this exam must be taken orally before an examination committee for the fourth and last time. The registration is done at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS).

retrieved.text: - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna, two diploma programmes remain: Law and Catholic Theology. These comprise between eight and ten semesters. Diploma programmes are divided into several sequential stages, which must be passed one after the other. The language of instruction of these degree programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Graduates are awarded the academic degree "Mag.iur." or "Mag.theol.". Related Link: - [Range of studies: bachelor and diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/) Related entries: - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Stage of the degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31266&cHash=a31be547fd42def4414091d3fe87e1d1) - Diploma Supplement Together with the degree certificate graduates receive a Diploma Supplement. The bilingual (German/English) Diploma Supplement aims at increasing the transparency and recognition of higher education programmes. It includes details on the kind and level of qualification as well as information about the completed degree programme (requirements, course of study, grading scale) and the higher education system in Austria. retrieved.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
retrieved.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

retrieved.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

reranked.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

- Board examination

  If you have not passed an exam three times, this exam must be taken orally before an examination committee for the fourth and last time. The registration is done at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS).

reranked.text: - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna, two diploma programmes remain: Law and Catholic Theology. These comprise between eight and ten semesters. Diploma programmes are divided into several sequential stages, which must be passed one after the other. The language of instruction of these degree programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Graduates are awarded the academic degree "Mag.iur." or "Mag.theol.". reranked.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
reranked.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

reranked.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

[### alphabetical order](#c285425)

[### by topic](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - General university entrance qualification You can only be admitted to a degree programme if you provide proof of the general university entrance qualification. Eligible proofs: Secondary-school leaving certificate, completion of a degree programme with a duration of at least three years at a recognised post-secondary educational institution (e.g. a university). Related Links: - [Admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung (university entrance qualification examination)](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [First-time admission](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30866&cHash=d40aa40f71c9ead7df1d946461e81e3d) - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31976&cHash=b71951a4fb2bb2fc18957130a051ca32) - German language proficiency/courses When applying for admission to all bachelor/diploma and German-language master degree programmes, you need recognised proof of your German language skills at level A2. In order to achieve the C1 level required for admission to a degree programme, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU). The Language Center of the University of Vienna is one of the cooperation partners of the VWU. Registration for the courses at the VWU and its co-operation partners is only possible with a letter of admission. In this letter, you will be required to take the supplementary German examination if you have not yet provided proof of C1 level at the time of application. You may also be required to take other supplementary subject-specific examinations. You must pass these before you can be admitted to the degree programme Related Links: - [Studying in the University Preperation Programme of the Vienna

selected.title: Additional exams
selected.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. selected.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
selected.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

- Board examination
